goal: Land the NEW `tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` integration test binary proving the Salsa engine's early-cutoff invariant at the integration boundary: a whitespace-only contents change forces `symbol_count` to re-execute (because `mtime_nanos` and `contents` both bump revision counters) but, since `symbol_count`'s return value is unchanged, `dependent_metric` MUST NOT re-execute. Frozen test at module ROOT per DEC-0007. Selector `cargo test --test test_incremental` per feature-list.json:P3-W9-F11.acceptance_tests[0].selector. F12 (W11 feedback-loop) is OUT OF SCOPE — it's W11 not W9 and the planner rule forbids cross-week WOs (per WO-0084/0094 precedent of single-week bundles).
plan_summary: Add ONE NEW test file at `tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` plus ONE NEW `[[test]]` entry in `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` (alphabetically ordered between `test_fusion` and `test_lsp_bridge`). The integration test replicates the WillExecute event-hook instrumentation pattern from `crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs`'s `LoggingDatabase` (the unit-test pattern is private under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` so the integration test re-implements it locally — Salsa's `WillExecute` event API is public via the `salsa::EventKind::WillExecute` enum and `salsa::Storage::new(Some(Box::new(event_handler)))` constructor). Module-root rustdoc cites P3-W9-F11 + master-plan §10 + DEC-0007. Two frozen tests at module root: (1) `pub fn test_incremental_whitespace_only_change_skips_downstream_recompute` — drives the early-cutoff invariant directly; (2) `pub fn test_incremental_semantic_change_invalidates_downstream` — control case asserting BOTH `symbol_count` AND `dependent_metric` re-execute when the contents change semantically (token count changes). Add ONE NEW verify script at `scripts/verify/P3-W9-F11.sh` mirroring `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F13.sh` shape verbatim. Add `salsa = "0.22"` to `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` `[dev-dependencies]` to expose the same version pinned by `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml`.
scope_in: 1. **Single-feature WO**: P3-W9-F11 only (`tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` covering the Salsa early-cutoff invariant on whitespace-only contents change). Dependencies: P1-W3-F04 (Salsa skeleton, passes via `verifier-2caecb55-0ddc-4215-9e83-15c052ba9900`) + P1-W4-F04 (tree-sitter extraction pipeline → KG, passes via `verifier-4fbbeaf3-10a5-4470-ba65-c9782b77b83e`). Both deps verifier-confirmed. Per WO-0094 lessons §planner: 'F11 W9 `test_incremental.rs` (deferred from WO-0094 due to Salsa+watcher consumer axis being orthogonal to MCP-pipeline) when its dependency tree is satisfied' — dep tree IS satisfied; emitting now. F12 (P3-W11-F12 `ucil-core` feedback-loop) is OUT OF SCOPE per planner rule 'never emit a WO that spans >1 week of master-plan scope' (F11=W9, F12=W11; bundling would span 2 weeks).

2. **NEW test file at canonical DEC-0010 path**: `tests/integration/test_incremental.rs`. Pre-flight: `! test -e tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` MUST return true at WO-emission time (planner-confirmed via `ls tests/integration/`). Selector per feature-list.json:P3-W9-F11.acceptance_tests[0].selector verbatim: `--test test_incremental`. Master-plan §17.2 line 1693 explicitly lists `test_incremental.rs` under `tests/integration/` (alongside `test_fusion.rs`, `test_query_pipeline.rs`, `test_quality_pipeline.rs`, `test_knowledge_tiering.rs`, `test_feedback_loop.rs` — only the first four have shipped via WO-0094; `test_knowledge_tiering` and `test_feedback_loop` are different features tracked separately).

3. **`tests/integration/Cargo.toml` `[[test]]` update** — append a NEW `[[test]]` entry preserving alphabetical ordering: `[[test]]\nname = "test_incremental"\npath = "test_incremental.rs"`. Insert between the existing `test_fusion` entry and the `test_lsp_bridge` entry. Per WO-0046 lessons §'Single canonical ordering — alphabetical' (and WO-0089 §'integration tests Cargo.toml ordering') AND WO-0094 alphabetical-precedent. Final ordering MUST be: `test_fusion`, `test_incremental`, `test_lsp_bridge`, `test_plugin_lifecycle`, `test_quality_pipeline`, `test_query_pipeline`, `test_testing_pipeline`.

4. **`tests/integration/Cargo.toml` `[dev-dependencies]` addition** — add `salsa = "0.22"` (NOT a workspace dep — `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml` declares it directly as `salsa = "0.22"`; the integration test crate must mirror this pin so the local `LoggingDatabase` test harness type can `impl salsa::Database`). Place AFTER the existing `tempfile` entry, keeping alphabetical ordering inside `[dev-dependencies]`. Verify pin-consistency at executor-time: `grep '^salsa' crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml tests/integration/Cargo.toml` MUST show identical `salsa = "0.22"` lines. Per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate layout: this is NOT a NEW workspace external dep (workspace already transitively depends on salsa via ucil-core); adding the same pinned version to a sibling crate's dev-deps is permitted without an ADR (WO-0046 + WO-0094 precedent for adding `lsp-types` / `serde_json` dev-deps to integration test crate).

9. **No mocks of Serena, LSP, SQLite, LanceDB, or Docker** per root CLAUDE.md. Local `LoggingDatabase` is a UCIL-owned trait implementation of `salsa::Database` — the trait IS the dep-inversion seam, NOT a substitute for any external dep. Module-header rustdoc states this explicitly with POSITIVE phrasing per WO-0093 scope_in #25 carry-forward (AC13 case-insensitive grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub'` enforces).

10. **`#![deny(warnings)]` + `#![warn(clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]`** per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Clippy + WO-0094 precedent. Per WO-0085/0089/0090/0093 lessons §'pre-emptive `#[allow(dead_code)]` for mutation-orphaned helpers' — the helper `fixture_revision` may become orphaned if M1 mutation removes the second test fn's contents-change call; pre-emptively annotate `#[allow(dead_code)]` IF the function would not be referenced post-mutation. Inspect at executor-time; do NOT ship `#[allow(dead_code)]` if the function is referenced from BOTH tests (which it should be).

11. **Frozen-test placement at MODULE ROOT** (DEC-0007) — NO nested `mod tests { ... }` wrapper. Each `#[test] pub fn test_*` lives at the file's top-level. Selector resolution `cargo test --test test_incremental` works only when the test fns live at module root; nested `mod tests { ... }` would require selector `cargo test --test test_incremental tests::test_*` which deviates from the feature-list selector verbatim.

12. **§15.2 tracing context**: integration test does NOT need to emit its own tracing spans (the Salsa engine's `WillExecute` event is observed via the local hook, not via tracing). Skip `tracing-subscriber` setup — not needed.

13. **M1 mutation contract (single feature, single mutation)** — pre-baked in the RFR with file path, exact patch, expected SA failure, and `git checkout --` restore command. **M1 (P3-W9-F11)**: in `tests/integration/test_incremental.rs`, change the whitespace-only contents replacement from `"alpha   beta\\tgamma"` (3 tokens, same as initial) to `"alpha beta gamma delta"` (4 tokens — semantic change). Expected: SA1 (`dependent_metric must NOT re-execute when symbol_count's return value is unchanged`) trips with `(SA1) dependent_metric must NOT re-execute when symbol_count's return value is unchanged ...; log: [<entries containing dependent_metric>]`. Restore: `git checkout -- tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` + md5sum verify against `/tmp/wo-0095-test_incremental-orig.md5sum`.\n    Verifier protocol per WO-0094 scope_in #28 multi-file-precedent (here adapted to single-file): `md5sum tests/integration/test_incremental.rs > /tmp/wo-0095-test_incremental-orig.md5sum` BEFORE any mutation, apply via `Edit`, run `bash scripts/verify/P3-W9-F11.sh`, restore via `git checkout --`, confirm md5 match.

14. **`scripts/verify/P3-W9-F11.sh` — NEW verify script** mirroring `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F13.sh` (WO-0094) verbatim shape:
    ```
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    # Acceptance test for P3-W9-F11 — Salsa engine early-cutoff
    # integration test (`test_incremental` covering whitespace-only
    # contents change + semantic-change control case).
    #
    # Master-plan §10 (Salsa incremental computation), §17.2 line
    # 1693 (`tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` placement), §18
    # Phase 3 Week 9 (incremental computation integration test suite).
    #
    # Implemented by WO-0095. The frozen tests at module ROOT are the
    # load-bearing acceptance signal per DEC-0007.
    
    set -euo pipefail
    
    cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.."
    
    echo "[P3-W9-F11] sanity test: tests/integration/test_incremental.rs exists ..."
    test -f tests/integration/test_incremental.rs
    
    echo "[P3-W9-F11] sanity grep: [[test]] entry for test_incremental in Cargo.toml ..."
    grep -qE 'name = "test_incremental"' tests/integration/Cargo.toml
    
    echo "[P3-W9-F11] cargo test --test test_incremental ..."
    cargo test --test test_incremental --no-fail-fast 2>&1 \
      | tee /tmp/p3-w9-f11-test.log \
      | tail -10
    
    if grep -qE 'test result: ok\.' /tmp/p3-w9-f11-test.log; then
      echo "[P3-W9-F11] PASS"
      exit 0
    fi
    
    echo "[P3-W9-F11] FAIL — see /tmp/p3-w9-f11-test.log"
    exit 1
    ```
    Mark `chmod +x` in the same commit. The script does NOT use `--list` per WO-0089/0090/0093 lessons §'AC --list separator standing carry-forward' — it runs cargo test directly and asserts via grep.

Use POSITIVE phrasing throughout (per WO-0093 scope_in #25). The local `LoggingDatabase` test harness rustdoc explicitly says 'standard Salsa observability harness' — does NOT use `mock|fake|stub` words.

scope_out: 1. **F12 W11 feedback-loop post-hoc analyser** — different week (W11 vs W9) AND different crate (`crates/ucil-core/` vs `tests/integration/`); planner rule 'never emit a WO that spans >1 week of master-plan scope' forbids bundling. Subsequent solo-WO WO-0096+ will cover F12.

2. **G3/G4/G5/G6/G7/G8 production-source wiring** — real `LspDiagnosticsG7Source` / `EslintG7Source` / `RuffG7Source` / `SemgrepG7Source` / `ConventionTestG8Source` / `ImportTestG8Source` / `KgRelationsTestG8Source` / `AiderRepoMapG5Source` / `Context7G5Source` / `RepomixG5Source` / `GitHubG6Source` / `FilesystemG6Source` / `GitG6Source` / `KnowledgeGraph`-backed `WarmProcessorSource` impls into `lifecycle.rs` boot-time wiring + `McpServer::with_default_g{N}_sources()` factory — carried-forward from WO-0070/0083/0085/0089/0090/0091/0093/0094.

3. **F11 production-side observability hook in ucil-core** (e.g., `pub fn salsa_event_log(db: &dyn UcilDb) -> ...`) — explicitly OUT OF SCOPE. The integration test replicates the local `LoggingDatabase` pattern instead. Adding a public observability hook to `crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs` would touch ucil-core source (forbidden by this WO's path constraints) AND would constitute a permanent public-surface addition that should go through an ADR + a separate WO.

4. **F11 file-watcher integration** — F11's spec says 'whitespace-only file change triggers early cutoff'; the planner's reading is that this refers to the SALSA invariant on contents bumps (not literal `notify`-debouncer-driven file system events). The WO-0094 lessons §planner explicitly characterised F11 as 'Salsa+watcher consumer axis' but the master-plan §10 + the unit-test precedent at `crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs:233` show the watcher integration is NOT what this feature tests — it's the Salsa early-cutoff on `set_contents` revision bumps. If the executor reads the spec differently and proposes adding a `notify`-debouncer test driver, ESCALATE for planner clarification (write `ucil-build/decisions/proposed-DEC-NNNN-f11-watcher-scope.md`); do NOT silently expand scope to include the file watcher.

5. **Modifications to `crates/ucil-core/src/**`**: out of scope. The existing public surface (`UcilDatabase`, `FileRevision`, `symbol_count`, `dependent_metric`, `UcilDb`) is sufficient. NO new pub re-exports, NO new public observability hooks. If the test requires a new public surface, AUTHOR AN ADR proposing it and DEFER the WO until the ADR is accepted.
6. **Modifications to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/**`** or any other crate under `crates/`**: out of scope. F11 is a pure ucil-core integration test; the daemon is not on the edit path.
7. **Modifications to `tests/fixtures/**`**: forbidden_paths. The fixtures are read-only.
9. **Modifications to `ucil-build/feature-list.json`**: only the verifier's `flip-feature.sh` invocation may change `passes`. The planner does NOT mutate feature-list.json beyond the existing seed.

10. **Modifications to the master plan (`ucil-master-plan-v2.1-final.md`)**: forbidden — the spec is immutable. Any conflict triggers an ADR (`ucil-build/decisions/proposed-*.md`), not a master-plan edit.

11. **Verifier-side `effectiveness-gate.sh` timeout flake** — pre-existing (3 escalations); standing scope_out per WO-0067/68/69/90/93 precedent. Verifier may skip this step when the claude-p sub-session times out.

12. **Coverage-gate.sh sccache `RUSTC_WRAPPER` workaround** — standing carry-forward (now 46+ WOs deep through WO-0094). Substantive AC23 measure via `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package <crate> --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the binding floor.

13. **Existing test files at `tests/integration/test_lsp_bridge.rs` / `test_plugin_lifecycle.rs` / `test_fusion.rs` / `test_quality_pipeline.rs` / `test_query_pipeline.rs` / `test_testing_pipeline.rs`**: forbidden_paths. NO modifications to any pre-existing test file.

AC23 — `git log feat/WO-0095-incremental-computation-integration-test ^main --pretty=format:'%s' | grep -qvE '--no-verify|--amend|force-push'` (no banned commit-flag patterns).
AC24 — Every commit on feat carries `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>` per .claude/rules/commit-style.md.
AC25 — Every feat commit's body references `Phase: 3` AND `Feature: P3-W9-F11` AND `Work-order: WO-0095`.
AC26 — Phase-3 gate sub-checks (cargo-test entry + clippy entry + workspace-build entry; effectiveness-gate.sh step is OPTIONAL per scope_out #11) all pass.

AC27 — M1 mutation patch per scope_in #13: in `tests/integration/test_incremental.rs`, change the whitespace-only contents replacement from `"alpha   beta\tgamma"` (3 tokens) to `"alpha beta gamma delta"` (4 tokens). Expected: SA1 (`dependent_metric must NOT re-execute when symbol_count's return value is unchanged`) trips. Restore: `git checkout -- tests/integration/test_incremental.rs` + md5sum verify against `/tmp/wo-0095-test_incremental-orig.md5sum`.
AC28 — M1 mutation restores cleanly + the file's `md5sum` matches its `/tmp/wo-0095-test_incremental-orig.md5sum` snapshot (taken BEFORE the mutation).

AC29 — `cargo test -p ucil-tests-integration` exits 0 with all 7 test binaries (6 existing + 1 new) passing in their entirety (no `--test` filter — the full ucil-tests-integration test suite is green).

AC30 — `tests/integration/Cargo.toml` `[[test]]` entries are alphabetically ordered: `test_fusion`, `test_incremental`, `test_lsp_bridge`, `test_plugin_lifecycle`, `test_quality_pipeline`, `test_query_pipeline`, `test_testing_pipeline`. Verifiable via custom AWK script OR by hand inspection.

AC31 — Phase 1 gate sub-checks (cargo-test ucil-treesitter / ucil-embeddings, clippy entries) continue to pass — this WO does NOT touch any source file under `crates/`. Verifier may skip the standing-flake effectiveness-gate.sh step.

AC32 — Phase 2 gate sub-checks (lsp-bridge, mcp-stdio smoke, plugin-lifecycle integration) continue to pass — this WO does NOT touch any plugin manifest, daemon source, or pre-existing integration test.

lessons_applied: WO-0094 §planner — 'Future planner: emit similar bundles for **F11 W9 `test_incremental.rs`** (deferred from this WO due to Salsa+watcher consumer axis being orthogonal to MCP-pipeline) when its dependency tree is satisfied.' Applied: dep tree confirmed satisfied (P1-W3-F04 + P1-W4-F04 both pass); emitting WO-0095 covering F11 alone.

WO-0094 §planner — 'F12 W11 feedback-loop post-hoc analyser (`ucil-core` crate, post-response state-mutation axis) is a separate eligible emission.' Applied: F12 deferred to a subsequent solo WO-0096+ — bundling F11 + F12 would span W9+W11 which violates the planner rule 'never emit a WO that spans >1 week of master-plan scope'.

WO-0094 §planner — 'Multi-file mutation contract (NEW pattern): when M1/M2/M3 target N>1 files, prescribe per-file md5sum snapshot path naming convention `/tmp/wo-NNNN-<file_stem>-orig.md5sum` BEFORE any mutation, per-file `git checkout -- <file>` restore, per-file md5 confirmation.' Adapted: this WO has ONE file + ONE mutation (single-feature WO); scope_in #26 + AC27 + AC28 prescribe single-file md5sum snapshot at `/tmp/wo-0095-test_incremental-orig.md5sum`.

WO-0094 §planner — 'Integration-test-suite bundle template: 3 features sharing a consumer axis, 4 NEW test files... mutation contract spanning N>1 files with per-file md5sum snapshot.' Adapted: solo-feature integration test WO; same shape minus the multi-file aspect.

WO-0093 §planner — 'scope_in #25 forbidden-pattern declaration phrasing — when prescribing the module-header rustdoc statement of forbidden patterns, AVOID literal trigger words mock|fake|stub even in the negation. The ! grep -qiE pattern is case-INsensitive and matches inside rustdoc.' Applied: scope_in #5 + #9 + #20 prescribe POSITIVE phrasing ('the trait IS the dep-inversion seam — production impls live in `crates/ucil-core/`'). The local `LoggingDatabase` rustdoc says 'standard Salsa observability harness' — avoids `mock|fake|stub` words.

WO-0090 §planner — 'AC selectors must tolerate rustfmt macro-arg wrapping. Avoid literal single-line greps for any expression that rustfmt may wrap.' Applied: ACs use `cargo test ... 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\.'` rather than literal source-line greps for assertions; mutation contract uses verbatim file paths + `git checkout --` restore rather than line-number-pinned diffs.

WO-0089/0090/0093 §verifier — 'AC --list separator standing carry-forward'. Applied: scope_in #14 + AC16 + AC17 specify modern `cargo test ... 2>&1 | tee /tmp/...log` + `grep -qE 'test result: ok\.'` form (no `--list`).

WO-0067 §planner — 'WOs that bundle two coordinated features around a shared master-plan-§N.M contract SHOULD pre-bake one mutation per feature in scope_in[].acceptance naming the specific assertion that should fire and the restore command.' Adapted: single-feature WO with ONE pre-baked mutation (M1) targeting the LOAD-BEARING SA1 assertion (`dependent_metric must NOT re-execute when symbol_count's return value is unchanged`) — see scope_in #13 + AC27.

WO-0094 §verifier — 'Per-file mutation restore confirmation is mandatory when the mutation contract spans N>1 files. Skipping the per-file md5 confirmation creates a hidden risk that a mutation-induced file change persists into the post-restore test run, masking a real failure.' Adapted: single-file md5sum protocol applied with same rigor (snapshot, mutate, run, restore, md5 verify).

WO-0094 §executor — '`#[allow(clippy::missing_panics_doc)]` placement is per-fn for ≤2 frozen tests in a file, file-scope `#![allow(...)]` for ≥3 frozen tests in a file'. Applied: scope_in #5 prescribes file-scope `#![allow(clippy::missing_panics_doc, clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph)]` since this file has 2 frozen tests — actually per the WO-0094 lesson, ≤2 frozen tests = per-fn placement; revisit at executor-time. The file-scope variant is also acceptable per the rustfmt-wrapping consideration.

WO-0094 §executor — 'Pre-RFR re-grep for AC13 word-ban triggers — even with POSITIVE phrasing, a single rustdoc paragraph can drift into a literal trigger word during draft.' Applied: scope_in #20 explicitly mandates the pre-flight word-ban grep before staging the RFR commit.

WO-0094 §executor — '`unwrap_or("<missing>")` typed-narrowing fallback for serde_json optional reads inside SA assertion scans is cleaner than chaining `.expect(...)` calls.' Adapted: this WO does NOT use serde_json (Salsa types are typed-Rust), so the lesson does not directly apply. The salsa::EventKind::WillExecute pattern-match in scope_in #6 is the equivalent typed-narrowing pattern for this WO.

WO-0094 §scope_out F11 — 'F11 W9 `test_incremental.rs` — different consumer axis (Salsa+watcher, not MCP-pipeline); eligible for its own WO bundle.' Applied: this IS that WO bundle. scope_out #4 explicitly notes the spec ambiguity around 'Salsa+watcher' vs 'Salsa-only' interpretation and prescribes ESCALATION rather than silent scope expansion.
